data.title: Collapse neutral surface palette to genuinely-used tokens

data.body: Separate PR from FRAC-200 (per user). Scope: collapse the leftover shadcn neutral palette to the tokens actually used by the editorial design. First step (this commit): make muted-foreground the single softer/secondary text color and remove foreground-light — migrate its 2 uses (NeighborhoodPage body + ordered list) to muted-foreground. Later steps to discuss: remove fully-dead card/popover/accent/destructive token pairs; fold secondary into muted; replace primary/primary-foreground (selection rule) with foreground/background; delete 6 dead section components (Directory/Events/PeopleDirectory/Projects/SkylineSilhouette/Vision) which is what makes secondary/muted dead. Branch based on frac-200.

Cold review (independent sub-agent, fresh context) of the 7-commit diff vs plan acceptance criteria: PASS. Verified: typecheck + build pass; tests exactly at FRAC-199 baseline (7 fail / 141 pass, same set); zero stragglers for all 14 removed/renamed tokens across src/ (only DESIGN.md 'renamed from' note matches); index.css defines exactly background/foreground/foreground-muted/foreground-faint (+house) and every border-foreground-faint/text-foreground-muted utility resolves; all 8 dead components deleted and imported nowhere; PeoplePage + /people route kept; no leftover temp preview routes; renames are value-preserving (foreground-muted=#525252, foreground-faint=#dddad5, selection/ring consolidations = #171717) so zero visual change except the intended gallery placeholder (bg-muted -> bg-foreground/5); DESIGN.md frontmatter+prose consistent (16 tokens). No implementation issues found.
